name: Justice for Walker
summary: Justice for Walker is a grassroots campaign calling for accountability and justice after the death of Warlpiri man Kumanjayi Walker.
notes: No clear standalone official website was found for the campaign itself; this is the most authoritative campaign-specific page located.

name: Pukatja Community
summary: Pukatja Community is an Anangu community on the APY Lands in north-west South Australia.
notes: Used the APY site community page because no distinct standalone website for a separate Pukatja Community Council was found.

name: National Disability Insurance Scheme
summary: The National Disability Insurance Scheme is Australia's national program that funds supports for eligible people with significant and permanent disability.
notes: Official Australian Government NDIS website.

name: Seed Mob
summary: Seed Mob is an Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander youth-led climate justice movement focused on protecting Country and communities.
notes: Official website of Seed Indigenous Youth Climate Network.

name: Pacific Australian Emerging Leaders Network
summary: The Pacific Australian Emerging Leaders Network connects young leaders across the Pacific and Australia for leadership, advocacy, and regional partnership.
notes: Network appears to be coordinated through Micah Australia rather than a separate standalone domain.

name: Murrie Connections
summary: Murrie Connections is an Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander-owned organisation that recruits culturally appropriate participants for research and consulting projects.
notes: Clear standalone official website found.

name: Mapoon Aboriginal Shire Council
summary: Mapoon Aboriginal Shire Council is the local government authority serving the Mapoon community in Cape York, Queensland.
notes: Official council domain.

name: Deadly Inspiring Youth Doing Good ATSI Corporation
summary: Deadly Inspiring Youth Doing Good ATSI Corporation is a youth-led First Nations not-for-profit that empowers young people through leadership and community programs.
notes: Official DIYDG website found with organisation details and contacts.

name: Aboriginal Community Connect
summary: Aboriginal Community Connect is a culturally focused support program helping Aboriginal people address alcohol and other drug issues while staying connected to culture and community.
notes: Appears to be a program under Uniting Communities rather than a separate incorporated entity.

name: Australian Progress
summary: Australian Progress builds the advocacy, leadership, and campaign capacity of social change organisations and civil society movements in Australia.
notes: Clear standalone official website found.

name: Thriving Families SA
summary: Thriving Families SA is a South Australian cross-sector initiative focused on prevention and early supports to improve outcomes for children, families, and communities.
notes: No clear standalone public website surfaced; LinkedIn appears to be the primary official profile.

name: Brisbane Blacks
summary: Brisbane Blacks appears to be a grassroots Aboriginal-led collective associated with community advocacy and political organising in Brisbane.
notes: Results were mostly social media and third-party references, and access to the Facebook page is restricted in search previews.

name: End Toxic Prisons Campaign
summary: End Toxic Prisons Campaign is an abolitionist campaign opposing youth incarceration and advocating community-controlled alternatives, especially for First Nations young people.
notes: Clear campaign website found.

name: First Nations Futures
summary: First Nations Futures is a First Nations-led economic justice organisation that redistributes flexible funding to First Nations community initiatives.
notes: Clear standalone official website found.

name: Woven Project
summary: Woven Project is an Indigenous-led climate justice initiative that builds global Indigenous collaboration, strategy, and advocacy for land rights and climate action.
notes: Search results strongly indicate this is the relevant entity, including references to a Common Threads co-hosted gathering in Australia.

name: Charles Campbell College
summary: Charles Campbell College is a South Australian government Reception-to-Year-12 school in Adelaide.
notes: Official school website found.

name: Digital Storytellers
summary: Digital Storytellers is an Australian social impact storytelling agency that helps purpose-driven organisations create narrative-led campaigns and media.
notes: Clear standalone official website found.

name: Gomeroi Traditional Owners Aboriginal Corporation
summary: This corporation entry represents an Aboriginal organisation connected to Gomeroi/Bigambul traditional owners and family groups.
notes: Could not find a clear standalone website for the exact listed name; closest authoritative result is an ACNC record with a closely related name.

name: Bama Connect Consultant
summary: Bama Connect Consultant provides culturally grounded consulting in Traditional Owner engagement, governance transition support, and on-Country facilitation.
notes: No standalone website surfaced; LinkedIn appears to be the primary official presence.

name: CFN
summary: CFN is a Queensland not-for-profit disability support provider delivering community participation, respite, and daily living services.
notes: CFN is acronym-ambiguous, but this was the strongest Australian organisational match with a complete official site.
